1.description: Evolution of support-reject differential. This figure plots simulated paths forN=40$N=40$,q=0.7$q=0.7$,p∗=V4=0.9673$p^{*}=V_{4}=0.9673$, and AoN thresholdT∗(N)=22$T^{*}(N)=22$. Case 1 indicates a path that crosses the cascade triggerk¯(p)+1=5$\bar{k}(p)+1=5$at the 26thagent, with all subsequent agents supporting regardless of their private signal. Case 2 indicates a path with no cascade, but the project is still funded by the end of the fundraising. Case 3 indicates a path where the AoN threshold is not reached and the project is not funded. The orange shaded region above the AoN line indicates that the project is funded. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Proposal profit as a function of price. This figure plots simulated expected profit as a function of price forN=2,000$N=2,000$,ν=0$\nu =0$, andq=0.55$q=0.55$. For each priceVk$V_k$, the threshold is set as the optimal one given the priceT=⌊N+k2⌋$T=\lfloor \frac{N+k}{2} \rfloor$. The optimalk*=13$k^\ast =13$, and the profit-maximizing price is around 0.93. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Cascades and optimal prices asNchanges. This figure shows the numerical solution of optimal price as a function ofNforν=0$\nu =0$. The blue plots represent the case in whichq=0.7$q=0.7$, and the orange plots represent the case in whichq=0.6$q=0.6$. Panel A plots the optimal priceVk$V_k$, while Panel B shows the correspondingk∗$k^*$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
